senses.gloss: for the sake of, because of

examples.text: أَنَا أَفْصَحُ العَرَبِ بَيْدَ أَنِّي مِنْ قُرَيْشٍ وَنَشَأْتُ فِي بَنِي سَعْدٍ
examples.english: My speech is the most clear among Arabs, for I'm from Quraysh and I grew up in Bani Sa'd.

examples.text: هُوَ كَثِيرُ المَالِ بَيْدَ أَنَّهُ بَخِيلٌ
نَحْنُ الآخِرُونَ السَّابِقُونَ يَوْمَ القِيَامَةِ بَيْدَ أَنَّهُمْ أُوتُوا الكِتَابَ مِنْ قَبْلِنَا وَأُوتِينَاهُ مِنْ بَعْدِهِمْ
examples.english: We are the latter, but the day of resurrection we will be first, even though they received the Scripture before us, and we received it after them.

body.text: , (T, S, M, L, Mughnee, K,) as also ↓
, (so in the Mughnee and in a MS. copy of the K and in the CK, and in a MS. copy of the K omitted,) a noun inseparably prefixed to

, (Ks, T, S, M, &c.,) but never otherwise than in the accus. case, nor as an epithet, nor otherwise than as an exceptive in a case in which the thing excepted is disunited in kind from that from which the exception is made. (Mughnee.) You say,

body.text_bw: huwa kaviyru AlmaAli bayoda A^an~ahu baxiylN
body.text_ar: هُوَ كَثِيرُ المَالِ بَيْدَ اَنَّهُ بَخِيلٌ

body.text: much, wealth, but he is niggardly.
(ISk, S, M, A, Msb, Mughnee.) ―         -b2-  Also as
, (M, K,) as some say; (A'Obeyd, M;) but to render it in the former manner is preferable. (M.) Accord. to some, (L,) it is syn. with

body.text_bw: naHonu AlA=xiruwna Als~aAbiquwna yawoma AlqyaAmapi bayoda A^an~ahumo A^uwtuw
body.text_ar: نَحْنُ الا=خِرُونَ السَّابِقُونَ يَوْمَ القيَامَةِ بَيْدَ اَنَّهُمْ اُوتُو

body.text_bw: AlkitaAba mino qabolinaA wa A^uwtiynaAhu mino baEodihimo
body.text_ar: الكِتَابَ مِنْ قَبْلِنَا وَ اُوتِينَاهُ مِنْ بَعْدِهِمْ

body.text: shall be those who will precede on the day of resurrection, although they were given the Scripture before us, and we were given it after them
]: (T, L:) El-Umawee holds it to be so: (T:) but Ks says that it here signifies
[as in the former ex.]: (T, L: [and so says IHsh in the Mughnee:]) accord. to one recital, it is
; so in the Musnad of the Imám Esh-Sháfi'ee: (Mughnee:) IAth says, I have not found this in the classical language in the sense of
we shall be those who will precede
on the day of resurrection by means of strength,
given us by God. (L.) ―         -b3-  Also, [accord. to some,] as
: (L, Mughnee, K:) as in the saying of Mohammad,

body.text_bw: A^anaA A^afoSaHu AlEarabi bayoda A^an~iY mino qurayo$K wana$aA^otu
body.text_ar: اَنَا اَفْصَحُ العَرَبِ بَيْدَ اَنِّى مِنْ قُرَيْشٍ وَنَشَاْتُ

body.text: I am the most chaste in speech of the Arabs because I am of the tribe of Kureysh and I grew up among the children of Saad
]: (T, L: [in the Mughnee given somewhat differently:]) but Ibn-Málik and others say that it here, also, means
, after the manner in which the latter is used in the saying [of a poet],

walaA Eayoba fiyhimo gayora A^an~a suyuwfahumo
                      
                     
                        bihin~a fuluwlN mino qiraAEi AlkataAy^ibi
[And there is no blemish in them, save that their swords have in them notches from the conflicting of the troops]. (Mughnee.) This manner of praising is termed by Abu-l-'Abbás Mohammad Ibn-Yezeed
is also a dial. var. of the same. (A'Obeyd, T, Mughnee.)
